action.reasoning: Observation: The GPS angle is consistently ±π (human directly behind robot). When I circle with lin_vel=5 + ang_vel=±15, the robot moves AWAY from human (dist goes 2.93→13m). The circle direction keeps flipping between +15 and -15 because angle oscillates between +π and -π at the boundary. Analysis: The fundamental fix is: when |angle| > π/2, SPIN IN PLACE (no forward motion). The forward motion while spinning causes the robot to orbit away. Also need to pick a consistent spin direction and not flip it. When angle is near ±π, I should commit to one direction (e.g., always spin left = +ang_vel) until angle < π/2. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with: 1) Pure spin when |angle| > π/2, 2) Committed spin direction that doesn't flip at ±π boundary, 3) Forward motion only when |angle| < π/2, 4) Submit eval immediately.
